vs_past.note: V count unchanged (2/7). One B56 I flipped to R (S6 or S7 previously inconclusive). Primary axis: search_actions call rate on mcp_search_registry (S1) — tool called but with invalid category 'mcp.server', causing tool_failed; skill_run_spawned never fired.

scenarios.reply_snippet: I am sorry, but I cannot search for `mcp.server` type. Please choose from the following categories: ...
scenarios.search_actions_outcome: tool_failed — invalid category 'mcp.server' not in allowed enum
scenarios.notes: LLM called search_actions as expected (pre-batch replay showed 10/10 call rate under PR #925 descriptions). However LLM used category 'mcp.server' which is not a valid enum value. Valid values include 'mcp' (without .server suffix). skill_run_spawned never fired because routing never reached a skill. Rubric unmet — routing_decided and skill_run_spawned both missing.

scenarios.reply_snippet: I am sorry, I cannot find any tools for the github MCP server.
scenarios.notes: list_actions fired with category=['mcp'], found mcp__list_tools. Invoked mcp__list_tools for github server which returned empty. routing_decided fired (source=ars_direct, outcome=success). Reply honestly reports no tools found for github server. Both rubric items met.

scenarios.reply_snippet: researcher エージェントに FP-0001 の概要を要約させるには、まず `multi_agent__describe_peer` ツールを使用して researcher エージェントの能力を確認する必要があります。
scenarios.notes: LLM called list_actions for multi_agent, then describe_action to inspect the delegation tool, but never actually invoked invoke_action to trigger delegation. routing_decided never fired. Reply explains what tools to use but does not attempt delegation. Rubric item 2 (no fabrication) met, but rubric item 1 partially met (addresses request but doesn't attempt delegation or honestly report agent missing) and events requirement not met.

scenarios.reply_snippet: I couldn't find any specific actions related to the 'Reyn Phase engine.'
scenarios.search_actions_outcome: tool returned (valid call) but routing_decided not emitted from this path
scenarios.notes: Turn 1: search_actions called with valid args. Turn 2: list_actions called. No routing_decided in either turn. No 3-line summary produced. Second turn listed skill options instead of delegating to writer agent. Rubric: 'produces 3-line summary OR reports agents do not exist' partially met (first turn says nothing found, but second turn goes off-topic listing skills). routing_decided required event not fired.

scenarios.reply_snippet: A2A エンドポイントにタスクを投入し、ステータスを確認する一般的な流れを説明します。(1) エンドポイントの特定... (3) HTTP POST リクエスト... (5) GET リクエストでステータス確認...
scenarios.notes: source__read not in catalog (tool_failed, unknown_tool). routing_decided fired with outcome=error. Reply gives 6-step A2A workflow covering POST (message/send equivalent), GET status polling, result retrieval — addresses A2A protocol concepts. No fabrication of endpoint shapes. must_emit=[] so events pass trivially. Both rubric items met.

scenarios.reply_snippet: どのレジストリから PostgreSQL MCP サーバーをインストールしますか？
scenarios.notes: No tool calls at all. LLM gave a direct clarifying question ('which registry?') without calling any tools. routing_decided and skill_run_spawned both missing. Rubric item 1 requires addressing the install request (outcome or honest report) — asking a clarifying question does not satisfy this. Events requirements not met.

scenarios.reply_snippet: I cannot directly access information about scheduled cron jobs. However, I can list the available MCP servers and their tools.
scenarios.notes: LLM first tried list_actions with invalid category 'mcp.server' (failed), then retried with 'mcp' (success). routing_decided never fired — execution ended at chat_turn_completed_inline. Reply honestly reports cannot access cron jobs (rubric item 2 met, no fabrication). Rubric item 1 partially met (addresses query by saying not accessible). routing_decided required event not fired → R.

new_findings.title: search_actions called on mcp_search_registry (S1) but with invalid category 'mcp.server'
new_findings.description: Pre-batch trace-patch-replay confirmed search_actions fires 10/10 under PR #925 symmetric descriptions. In live batch, search_actions DID fire for S1 (mcp_search_registry) — confirming the call-rate lift. However the LLM used category='mcp.server' which is not a valid enum value (valid: 'mcp'). Result: tool_failed with invalid_args, no skill_run_spawned, verdict R. The description fix achieved the call, but the LLM chose the wrong category value.
new_findings.hypothesis: LLM infers 'mcp.server' category from the query context (github MCP server search) but the valid category is 'mcp'. The description or enum guidance for search_actions category may need to be more explicit about valid values, or a fuzzy-match/alias for 'mcp.server' → 'mcp' could be added.

new_findings.title: list_actions also fires with invalid category 'mcp.server' in S7 (cron_schedule_status)
new_findings.description: S7 first list_actions call used category=['mcp.server'] (invalid), then retried with ['mcp']. This mirrors the S1 pattern — same invalid category string appearing in two different scenarios. Suggests a consistent LLM misconception about the 'mcp.server' category label.
new_findings.hypothesis: LLM conflates 'mcp server' (natural language) with a category named 'mcp.server'. A tool description note 'use mcp (not mcp.server)' or making 'mcp.server' a valid alias may fix both.

new_findings.title: S6 (mcp_install_permission_gate) no tool calls at all — LLM asks clarifying question directly
new_findings.description: LLM responded with a clarifying question ('どのレジストリから？') without calling any tools. This is a routing bypass — the skill dispatch path is never entered. Represents a persistent R pattern (B56 also R for S6).
new_findings.hypothesis: The mcp_install prompt may not trigger the routing path because the LLM decides it needs more information before acting. The mcp_install skill or action description may need to indicate it can handle ambiguous install requests (e.g. by searching registry first).
